blocks.body: Sentence Transformations — Word Order / Emphasis shows how to reshape word order for focus, inversion, cleft sentences, and emphatic forms.

{"level":"B2-C1","intro":"In word-order transformations, you are not changing the fact itself but the focus and rhetorical effect of the sentence: inversion, emphatic do, a cleft structure, or strong fronting with a restrictive meaning.","rules":[{"label":"EMPHASIS NEEDS CONTROL","color":"emerald","text":"Inversion and cleft sentences only work when you clearly see which element needs emphasis.","example":"It was Tom who broke the printer."},{"label":"AUXILIARY MATTERS","color":"blue","text":"After negative or restrictive expressions at the front, you often need inversion with an auxiliary.","example":"Never have I seen..."},{"label":"DON'T CHANGE THE MEANING","color":"amber","text":"An emphatic rewrite should not turn a neutral sentence into a different message. It should only change the focus or the force.","example":"I do want to help."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How to Spot the Needed Rewrite
blocks.body:
First decide what effect the new sentence should create.

  • Inversion often appears after never, rarely, under no circumstances, or only then.
  • Cleft sentence is useful when you need to highlight a person, thing, place, or time: It was ... who / that ....
  • Emphatic do strengthens a normal statement in the present or past: I do agree, She did call.
  • In a transformation task, check whether the new structure really emphasises the right element instead of just sounding more complicated.

blocks.heading: 2. Core Transformation Patterns
blocks.body:
Base sentenceTarget sentenceRule focus
I had never seen such a confusing task before.Never before had I seen such a confusing task.negative fronting + inversion.
Tom broke the printer, not me.It was Tom who broke the printer.cleft sentence to highlight the doer.
I really want to help.I do want to help.emphatic do for stronger assertion.
You should not press this button under any circumstances.Under no circumstances should you press this button.fronted negative phrase + inversion.

In these tasks, the rewritten sentence must be not only grammatically correct but also stylistically natural.

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Keeping normal word order after a fronted negative expression.","wrong":"Never before I had seen such a view.","right":"Never before had I seen such a view.","hint":"After these triggers, you often need inversion."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using do with the verb be or with a modal in the same way.","wrong":"I do am ready.","right":"I am ready. / I really am ready.","hint":"Emphatic do does not combine with be and modals in the same pattern."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Building a cleft sentence with no clear focus.","wrong":"It was yesterday that I arrived yesterday.","right":"It was yesterday that I arrived.","hint":"A cleft sentence should highlight one specific element, not repeat information."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Using inversion when there is no real trigger for it.","wrong":"Often do I visit my grandparents.","right":"I often visit my grandparents.","hint":"Not every fronted adverb needs inversion. Negative and restrictive triggers are the key ones."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick Exam Check","items":["Decide what needs emphasis: agent, object, time, restriction, or strength of assertion.","If the sentence starts with a negative or restrictive phrase, check whether inversion is required.","Do not add emphatic do automatically; it does not fit every structure.","Read the rewritten sentence aloud in your head: does it sound like natural emphasis rather than a random rearrangement?"]}
